name: Elysia (Bun-first TypeScript API Framework)

auth.notes: Framework for building APIs; auth via plugins (elysia-jwt, elysia-bearer)

performance.notes: Fastest JS framework benchmarks; Bun runtime; static code analysis for JIT optimization; >1M req/sec

agent_readiness.notes: Bun-first TypeScript API framework optimized for performance. End-to-end type safety with Eden Treaty client. TypeBox schema validation integrated. OpenAPI 3.0 generation with Swagger. Plugin system (elysia-jwt, elysia-cors, elysia-rate-limit). Guard, lifecycle hooks, macro. Static code analysis for JIT optimization — fastest JS HTTP framework in benchmarks. Comparable to Go/Rust performance on Bun. Growing ecosystem.

gotchas: Bun required for maximum performance (works on Node.js but slower)
Breaking changes between minor versions in v1 era
Less community resources than mature frameworks
best_when: End-to-end type safety with Eden Treaty client
Teams wanting Next-gen performance without Rust/Go
avoid_when: Mature ecosystem requirements (Express or Fastify have more plugins)
summary: Elysia is a Bun-first TypeScript HTTP framework achieving >1M req/sec through static code analysis and JIT optimization. End-to-end type safety with Eden Treaty client. TypeBox validation, OpenAPI generation, plugin system. Fastest JS framework in benchmarks — comparable to Go/Rust performance. Works on Node.js but Bun gives full performance. Growing ecosystem, younger than Express/Fastify.
